$schema: internal — supplement evidence grading data for the Supplement Evidence Grader tool
verificationNotes: Every entry verified live against PubMed by a research subagent on 2026-04-08. Re-verify annually per the staleness audit recommendation. Magnitudes are intentionally honest — most supplements show modest effects in calorie-restricted contexts; UNVERIFIED items are flagged rather than fabricated.

supplements.name: Berberine
supplements.mechanism: AMPK activation; gut microbiome modulation; insulin sensitivity
supplements.safetyConcerns: GI upset (~34%); CYP3A4 inhibition + statin interaction; avoid in pregnancy (bilirubin displacement / kernicterus risk)
supplements.confidenceNote: Real, replicable, well-characterized mechanism. ~1/7 of GLP-1 magnitude.

supplements.name: Green tea catechins / EGCG
supplements.mechanism: Catechin + caffeine COMT inhibition → norepinephrine prolongation → thermogenesis
supplements.safetyConcerns: Caffeine effects (insomnia, palpitations); rare hepatotoxicity at high-dose extracts (not from beverage)
supplements.confidenceNote: Decaffeinated forms minimal benefit. Modest but real.

supplements.name: Conjugated linoleic acid (CLA)

primaryStudy.weightEffect: −0.09 kg/wk (≈−4.7 kg over 6 months at 3.2 g/day)

supplements.safetyConcerns: GI upset (diarrhea, fatty stools); insulin resistance reported in diabetic subgroups; expensive
supplements.confidenceNote: Effect plateaus after 6 months. Cost-effectiveness marginal.

supplements.name: Glucomannan / konjac fiber
supplements.mechanism: Gel-forming viscous fiber → gastric volume expansion → satiety
supplements.safetyConcerns: Choking/esophageal obstruction if not taken with adequate water; initial GI bloating
supplements.confidenceNote: Cheap, safe, mechanical satiety. Reasonable adjunct.

supplements.name: Psyllium husk
supplements.mechanism: Gel-forming fiber → delayed gastric emptying → satiety + lipid binding
supplements.safetyConcerns: Choking risk if inadequate water; initial GI bloating; safe in renal disease
supplements.confidenceNote: Similar profile to glucomannan + meaningful LDL/triglyceride effects.

supplements.name: MCT oil (as LCT replacement)
supplements.mechanism: Rapid hepatic ketone production; modestly higher thermogenesis vs LCT
supplements.safetyConcerns: GI upset if dose escalated; high caloric density
supplements.confidenceNote: Works only as LCT replacement, NOT as additive caloric source. Bulletproof coffee ≠ this trial design.

supplements.name: Apple cider vinegar
supplements.latinName: Acetic acid (4-8%) in fermented apple juice
supplements.mechanism: Acetic acid delays gastric emptying; modulates glucose response

primaryStudy.weightEffect: Insufficient evidence for meaningful weight effect

supplements.safetyConcerns: Dental erosion with prolonged exposure; esophageal irritation if undiluted
supplements.confidenceNote: Khezri 2018 reported ~1.2 kg in calorie-restricted context (n=39, not PubMed-indexed). Abou-Khalil 2024 BMJ NPH adolescent trial RETRACTED Sept 2025.

supplements.name: Ashwagandha
supplements.mechanism: Cortisol reduction; HPA-axis modulation; sleep quality improvement

primaryStudy.journal: J Evid Based Complement Altern Med
primaryStudy.weightEffect: Directional improvement; exact weight effect not disclosed in abstract

supplements.safetyConcerns: Rare hepatotoxicity case reports; avoid in pregnancy; thyroid medication interaction
supplements.confidenceNote: Single underpowered trial (n=52). Cortisol reduction better evidenced (Salve 2019, Lopresti 2019) but cortisol ≠ weight loss.

supplements.name: Magnesium
supplements.mechanism: Insulin sensitivity, inflammation reduction, metabolic enzyme cofactor

primaryStudy.weightEffect: BMI −0.21 kg/m² (ns); waist −2.09 cm in obese subgroup

supplements.safetyConcerns: GI upset (diarrhea) at high doses; safe in renal monitoring
supplements.confidenceNote: Weight-neutral overall. Useful for muscle cramps, sleep, insulin sensitivity in deficient adults.

supplements.name: Cinnamon
supplements.mechanism: Insulin sensitivity; modest fasting glucose reduction

primaryStudy.weightEffect: Not reported as primary outcome; no weight effect in available meta data

supplements.safetyConcerns: Coumarin content (cassia) at high doses; anticoagulant interaction at very high intake
supplements.confidenceNote: Modest fasting glucose effect (−24.6 mg/dL); no meaningful weight effect.

supplements.name: Chromium picolinate

primaryStudy.authors: Onakpoya I, Posadzki P, Ernst E

supplements.confidenceNote: Effect quality-dependent; magnitude unclear and clinically marginal.

supplements.name: Creatine monohydrate
supplements.mechanism: Phosphocreatine for ATP regeneration; lean mass preservation in caloric deficit

primaryStudy.weightEffect: Lean mass +0.68 kg; total body weight +0.86 kg (water retention initially)

supplements.safetyConcerns: Increases creatinine (lab marker); safe in healthy renal function
supplements.confidenceNote: Creatine INCREASES scale weight short-term via water retention. Excellent for muscle preservation paired with resistance training; NOT a weight-loss intervention.

supplements.name: Collagen peptides
supplements.mechanism: Skin elasticity (Proksch 2014); joint health; protein satiety

primaryStudy.weightEffect: Skin elasticity improved; weight not measured

supplements.confidenceNote: Useful for skin elasticity in mid-life women; useless for weight loss directly. Relevant for loose-skin concern after rapid GLP-1 loss.

supplements.name: Garcinia cambogia (HCA)
supplements.mechanism: Citrate lyase inhibition; serotonergic appetite suppression (claimed)

primaryStudy.weightEffect: −0.88 kg (95% CI −1.75 to 0.00) — disappears under quality filter

supplements.safetyConcerns: Rare hepatotoxicity case reports (FDA Consumer Update 2009)
supplements.confidenceNote: Heavily marketed; effect disappears with quality filtering. Hepatotoxicity signal too small to justify any risk.

supplements.name: Lemon balm
supplements.mechanism: GABA-A modulation (anxiolytic); claimed indirect via stress reduction

primaryStudy.authors: Heshmati J et al. (no weight outcomes)

supplements.confidenceNote: Zero human RCTs for weight loss. Useful for anxiety; marketing volume vastly exceeds evidence.

supplements.name: L-Lysine
supplements.safetyConcerns: Generally safe; GI effects at high doses
supplements.confidenceNote: >94% of US adults already meet lysine requirements from diet. Marketing claim not evidence-supported.
